message:
docs(readme+robot): surface `cass sources agents {list|exclude|include}` in README quick start and robot-docs sources topic

Document the persisted-exclusion feature just shipped. The
`disabled_agents` config + `cass sources agents …` CLI landed in
commit 82d8d70e, but were only reachable via
`cass robot-docs sources` and were not referenced from the main README
quick-start or the human-readable robot-docs landing — users coming
from the top-level docs had no way to discover them.

README.md
- Add a 3-line entry to the getting-started quick-start block showing
  `list --json` / `exclude openclaw` / `include openclaw`.
- Add a dedicated "Excluding Noisy Agent Harnesses" subsection under
  the existing Sources docs that:
  * Explains when to reach for it (harness generating mostly junk /
    looped output).
  * Gives the three invocations (list/exclude/include).
  * Calls out where the preference is persisted
    (`~/.config/cass/sources.toml` on Linux,
    `~/Library/Application Support/cass/sources.toml` on macOS) so
    users understand it survives restarts and participates in future
    scans/syncs/watch-mode runs.
  * Documents the default "also purges archived local data" behavior
    and the `--keep-indexed-data` escape hatch for users who want to
    block future indexing without reclaiming disk.

src/lib.rs (robot-docs)
- `print_robot_docs(RobotTopic::Commands)` (the human-readable
  landing) now mentions `cass robot-docs sources` alongside
  `robot-docs commands | examples`, and calls out that it covers
  "remote sync/setup plus persistent agent-harness exclusions" so
  agents discover the exclusion CLI without having to know the
  feature name up front.
- `print_robot_docs(RobotTopic::Examples)` gains a "Persistently
  exclude a noisy harness from indexing" block with the four common
  invocations (`list --json`, `exclude openclaw`, `exclude openclaw
  --keep-indexed-data`, `include openclaw`).

Pure docs change — no behavior change.

files.patch:
@@ -67,6 +67,11 @@ cass expand /path/to/session.jsonl -n 42 -C 3 --json
 # 5) Discover the full machine API
 cass robot-docs guide
 cass robot-docs schemas
+
+# 6) Exclude a noisy agent harness from future indexing
+cass sources agents list --json
+cass sources agents exclude openclaw
+cass sources agents include openclaw
 ```
 
 **Output conventions**
@@ -533,6 +538,31 @@ cass sources doctor [--source <name>] [--json]
 cass sources sync [--source <name>] [--no-index] [--verbose] [--dry-run] [--json]
 ```
 
+#### Excluding Noisy Agent Harnesses
+
+If one harness is generating mostly junk or looped output, you can disable it persistently even if its files remain on disk:
+
+```bash
+# Inspect current include/exclude state
+cass sources agents list --json
+
+# Stop indexing this harness in future runs
+cass sources agents exclude openclaw
+
+# Re-enable it later
+cass sources agents include openclaw
+```
+
+`cass` stores this preference in `sources.toml` (`~/.config/cass/sources.toml` on Linux, `~/Library/Application Support/cass/sources.toml` on macOS), so future scans, syncs, and watch-mode updates remember it automatically.
+
+By default, `cass sources agents exclude <agent>` also removes already archived local data for that agent and rebuilds the lexical index so the exclusion frees space instead of only blocking future imports.
+
+If you want to block future indexing but keep the data already archived:
+
+```bash
+cass sources agents exclude openclaw --keep-indexed-data
+```
+
 #### Sync Engine Internals
 
 The sync engine uses rsync over SSH for efficient delta transfers, with automatic SFTP fallback:
